transcript_facts:
*   The patient reports fatigue for the past two months.
*   The patient denies blood in stool.
*   The clinician suspects cervical radiculopathy.
*   The clinician plans a cervical MRI.
*   The patient reports a cough for about three months.
*   The patient reports a cough started around the time he was loading bills.
*   The patient reports a cold preceded the cough.
*   The patient exercises regularly, specifically biking.
*   The patient mentions the new rail trail opened sixty miles long.
*   The patient prefers riding on the rail trails.
*   The patient has a history of rheumatoid arthritis.
*   The patient takes methotrexate seven and a half milligrams weekly.
*   The patient reports his joints feel okay with activity and occasional hand problems.
*   The patient stopped smoking seven years ago.
*   The patient states he felt better after stopping smoking.
*   The patient denies any pain or shortness of breath.
*   The patient has no family history of lung cancer.
*   The patient denies any previous lung infections or pneumonia.
*   The doctor's blood pressure is 124/76.
*   The doctor's heart rate is 70.
*   The doctor's respiration rate is 16.
*   The patient's oxygen saturation is 98% on room air.
*   The patient is afebrile.
*   The doctor auscultates clear and equal bilateral lungs.
*   The doctor auscultates a regular rate and rhythm heart.
*   The doctor finds no clicks, rubs, or murmurs.
*   The patient has some swelling to his knuckles on the third digit of each hand.
*   The CT scan shows a solitary two centimeter nodule in the right upper lobe.
*   The nodule is smooth and shows no emphysematous disease.
*   The clinician orders a pulmonary function test (PFT).
*   The clinician orders a PET CT.
*   The clinician suggests video assisted thoracoscopy removal of the nodule.
*   The patient will be under general anesthesia during the procedure.
*   The procedure is expected to take about an hour and a half.
*   The patient will be admitted for one night.

- The patient reports fatigue for the past two months.

- The patient prefers riding on the rail trails.
- The patient has a history of rheumatoid arthritis.
- The patient takes methotrexate seven and a half milligrams weekly.
- The patient reports his joints feel okay with activity and occasional hand problems.
- The patient stopped smoking seven years ago.
- The patient states he felt better after stopping smoking.
